spec_match.rationale: The live probe now checks `CSS.supports('flex-wrap', 'balance')`, reports the baseline `wrap` parser result, and uses an `@supports` fallback that keeps unsupported browsers readable.

interactive.rationale: The concept has a card-width slider, base labels-per-card slider, custom label editor that visibly adds labels to every card, custom-label chips, and live row-count readouts for both wrap and balance panels.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The card container concept matches the target use case, showing how labels wrap on grid articles.

edge_cases.rationale: Variable card widths, tag counts, custom labels, duplicate-label handling, empty-input feedback, reset behavior, and resize-driven row recounting exercise narrow cards, longer labels, and differing breakpoints.

references.rationale: Cites the ChromeStatus feature entry and links the CSS Flexible Box Layout Level 2 draft used by the demo.

design_system.rationale: Uses design-system variables, avoids raw hex colours, keeps button background/color inheritance safe, and remains an interactive comparison rather than a static code card.

openQuestions.title: Verify Add label in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to add a custom label, confirm the label appears as a chip and inside every wrap/balance panel, confirm duplicate and empty-input status messages, and run the feature conformance page in the same browser.

summary: Resolved the add-label issue: custom labels now render immediately in every card instead of being hidden behind the base-label slice, and the controls expose the added labels and status.
